sections.paragraph:
ἅρπαξ, αγος (ἁρπάζω) adj. (Hes.+; X., LXX; TestBenj 11:1; Ar. 8:2; Just., A. II, 2, 16; Theoph. Ant. 1, 2 [p. 60, 27]; in a vice list).

① rapacious, ravenous of wolves (Gen 49:27) Mt 7:15.

② subst., ὁ ἅ. robber (Jos., Bell. 6, 203; PRossGeorg V, 22, 16); to differentiate it fr. λῃστής perh. better swindler or rogue (Dssm., LO 269, 4 [LAE 321, 1]; ἅρπαξ and λῃστής in juxtaposition: Artem. 4, 56) w. ἄδικοι, μοιχοί Lk 18:11; cp. 1 Cor 5:10f; 6:10; Tit 1:9 v.l.; D 2:6.—DELG s.v. ἁρπάζω. M-M.

adj. adj. = adjective

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PRossGeorg PRossGeorg = Papyri russischer u. georgischer Sammlgen—List 4

  1. fr. = from

perh. perh. = perhaps

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

LO LO = Licht vom Osten—s. Dssm., LO in List 6

LAE LAE , s. Dssm. LO—List 6

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 134
